2605.21060.title: Divide et Calibra: Multiclass Local Calibration via Vector Quantization
2605.21060.paper_summary: Accurate and well-calibrated Machine Learning (ML) models are mandatory in high-stakes settings, yet effective multiclass calibration remains challenging: global approaches assume calibration errors are homogeneous across the latent space, while local methods often rely on latent-space dimensionality reduction, which leads to information loss. To address these issues, we propose a compositional approach to multiclass calibration, where region-specific calibration maps are constructed from shared codeword-dependent factors. We instantiate this idea via Vector Quantization (VQ), which induces a structured partition of the representation space, and an indexed parameterization of Dirichlet concentrations that enables parameter sharing across regions. Our approach learns heterogeneous calibration maps that generalize well even to sparse regions of the latent space. Experiments on benchmark datasets show significant improvements in local calibration while maintaining competitive global calibration and predictive performance.
2605.21060.paper_summary_zh: 準確且經過良好校準的機器學習（ML）模型在高風險環境中是必須的，但有效的多類別校準仍然具有挑戰性：全局方法假設校準誤差在潛在空間中是均勻的，而局部方法則通常依賴於潛在空間的降維，這會導致信息損失。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了一種多類別校準的組合方法，其中根據共享的代碼字依賴因子構建區域特定的校準圖。我們通過向量量化（VQ）實現這一想法，該方法引入了表示空間的結構化劃分，以及一種指數參數化的Dirichlet濃度，使得跨區域的參數共享成為可能。我們的方法學習異質的校準圖，即使在潛在空間的稀疏區域也能很好地泛化。在基準數據集上的實驗顯示，在保持競爭性的全局校準和預測性能的同時，局部校準有顯著改善。
2605.21060.author: Cesare Barbera et.al.
2605.21060.authors: Cesare Barbera, Lorenzo Perini, Giovanni De Toni, Andrea Passerini, Andrea Pugnana
